description: Part 5 of Flashcards Set 2 (Policy provisions, group & health insurance basics)

questions.question: An insured covered by life insurance has just died. What will happen if the primary beneficiary had already died before the insured and contingent beneficiary?
options: Proceeds will go to the primary beneficiary's estate
Probate will decide who receives proceeds
Proceeds will go to the contingent beneficiary
Proceeds will go to the insured's estate
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Identify what the question is asking (rule, calculation, or outcome).
Step 2: Apply the LLQP rule or formula that governs the scenario.
Step 3: Eliminate distractors using the facts and remove options that conflict with the scenario.
Step 4: Select the best answer. Option C: Proceeds will go to the contingent beneficiary.
optionFeedback: "Proceeds will go to the primary beneficiary's estate" is a different beneficiary designation than the one this question is testing. The correct answer is "Proceeds will go to the contingent beneficiary" because a contingent beneficiary is paid only if the primary beneficiary dies before the insured.

"Probate will decide who receives proceeds" is a different beneficiary designation than the one this question is testing. The correct answer is "Proceeds will go to the contingent beneficiary" because a contingent beneficiary is paid only if the primary beneficiary dies before the insured.
Proceeds will go to the contingent beneficiary.

"Proceeds will go to the insured's estate" is a different beneficiary designation than the one this question is testing. The correct answer is "Proceeds will go to the contingent beneficiary" because a contingent beneficiary is paid only if the primary beneficiary dies before the insured.

questions.question: N is covered by a Term Life policy and does not make the required premium payment which was due August 1. N dies September 15. What action will the insurer take?
options: Claim will be paid in full
Claim will be decided by an arbitrator
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Identify what the question is asking (rule, calculation, or outcome).
Step 2: Apply the LLQP rule or formula that governs the scenario.
Step 3: Eliminate distractors using the facts and remove options that conflict with the scenario.
Step 4: Select the best answer. Option A: Claim will be denied.
optionFeedback: Claim will be denied.
"Claim will be paid in full" is not the exception here. It is one of the items that is normally true or required. The better answer here is "Claim will be denied".
"Claim will be partially paid" is not the exception here. It is one of the items that is normally true or required. The better answer here is "Claim will be denied".
"Claim will be decided by an arbitrator" is not the exception here. It is one of the items that is normally true or required. The better answer here is "Claim will be denied".

questions.question: E and F are business partners. Each takes out a $500,000 life insurance policy on the other, naming himself as primary beneficiary. E and F eventually terminate their business, and four months later E dies. Although E was married with three children at the time of death, the primary beneficiary is still F. However, an insurable interest no longer exists. Where will the proceeds from E's life insurance policy be directed to?
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Identify what the question is asking (rule, calculation, or outcome).
Step 2: Apply the LLQP rule or formula that governs the scenario.
Step 3: Eliminate distractors using the facts and remove options that conflict with the scenario.
Step 4: Select the best answer. Option A: F.
optionFeedback: This option best matches the definition, rule, or outcome the question is testing. Where will the proceeds from E's life insurance policy be directed to?.
"The dissolved partnership" is a different statement than the one this question is testing. The better answer here is "F".
"E's family" is a different beneficiary designation than the one this question is testing. The better answer here is "F".
"E's estate" is a different beneficiary designation than the one this question is testing. The better answer here is "F".

questions.question: Which of these actions should a producer take when submitting an insurance application to an insurer?
options: Issue a binding receipt to applicant if no initial premium is submitted
Disclose to the applicant the amount of commissions to be earned on this transaction
Inform insurer of relevant information not included on the application
Arrange for a copy of the Attending Physician Statement (APS) to be sent to the producer
questions.explanation:
Step 1: Identify what the question is asking (rule, calculation, or outcome).
Step 2: Apply the LLQP rule or formula that governs the scenario.
Step 3: Eliminate distractors using the facts and remove options that conflict with the scenario.
Step 4: Select the best answer. Option C: Inform insurer of relevant information not included on the application.
optionFeedback: "Issue a binding receipt to applicant if no initial premium is submitted" is a different document than the one this question is testing. The correct answer is "Inform insurer of relevant information not included on the application" because that action follows the underwriting or delivery rule being tested in this scenario.

"Disclose to the applicant the amount of commissions to be earned on this transaction" is a different application requirement than the one this question is testing. The correct answer is "Inform insurer of relevant information not included on the application" because that action follows the underwriting or delivery rule being tested in this scenario.
Inform insurer of relevant information not included on the application.

"Arrange for a copy of the Attending Physician Statement (APS) to be sent to the producer" is a different application requirement than the one this question is testing.
